


<body>
 
<p class="paragraph-P1">The publication of NI-CPU-E</p>
<p class="paragraph-P1"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P2"><span class="text-T1">the CPU A</span>rchitecture <span class="text-T1">is simulated in </span><a href="https://github.com/logisim-evolution/logisim-evolution#download" class="text-Internet_20_link"><span class="text-T1">Logisim evulution</span></a><span class="text-T1">.</span></p>

<p class="paragraph-P3">The logisim file can be found <a href="https://github.com/Nilomat/NI_CPUE2G/blob/main/cpue2g/cpu_e2.circ" class="text-Internet_20_link">here</a>.</p>
<p class="paragraph-P4"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P5">SPECS</p>
<p class="paragraph-P6"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P7">The CPU_E series is a <span class="text-T2">non </span>turing complete 8bit <span class="text-T2">risc </span>processor.</p>

<p class="paragraph-P7">With 2-4 multi purpose registers and 2 ALU registers</p>

<p class="paragraph-P7">and 65k of rom. It also has 255 bytes of ram.</p>
<p class="paragraph-P7"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P8">THE  <span class="text-T3">ALU </span></p>
<p class="paragraph-P7"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P7">It consists of an addition subtraction unit</p>

<p class="paragraph-P7">and the comparetors larger than smaller than and equals.</p>

<p class="paragraph-P7">It also has the logical operation XOR OR and AND.</p>
<p class="paragraph-P7"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P8">THE<span class="text-T3"> I/O</span></p>
<p class="paragraph-P7"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P7">It has 4 input and 4 output ports.</p>

<p class="paragraph-P7">The general counter x/y(gcx/gcy) <span class="text-T4">are also i/o ports</span>.</p>

<p class="paragraph-P7">Each I/O port is 8 bits (including gcx/gcy).</p>
<p class="paragraph-P7"> </p><p class="paragraph-P5"> </p><p class="paragraph-P9"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P10">This is a cpu wich is now open source. This cpu was closed source for about 1 year after its designing. Now its open source since it has a successor. I make my cpus public when there is a succesor. since the successor is often so much faster than the old version it seems obsolete to me. this is because the first 2 cpus ( ni_cpu 3/4 and NI_CPUE2G ) where verry limited. the biggist limit of NI_CPUE2G is the memmory structure wich doesnt allow you to directly transfer data betwenn points. this means usign more than one value at a time is difficult to impossible. it also was missing important alu instruktions. so both old open source cpus where verry basic.</p>

<!--Next 'div' was a 'text:p'.-->
<div class="paragraph-P11">
<!--Next 'div' is a draw:frame. -->

<p class="paragraph-P12"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P12"><a href="https://nilomat.github.io/NI_CPUE2G/" class="text-Internet_20_link">NI_CPUE2G</a></p>
<p class="paragraph-P12"> </p>
<p class="paragraph-P12"><a href="https://nilomat.github.io/NI_CPUE2G/cpue2g/NI_CPUE2G%20datasheet.pdf" class="text-Internet_20_link">NI_CPUE2G datasheet</a></p>
</body>
